cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem step by step. So, the question is about Jane's birthday and figuring out a specific date one year ago from today. Let me start by understanding all the given information. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Now, the last day of February depends on whether it's a leap year or not. Leap years occur every 4 years, right? So, February has 29 days in a leap year and 28 otherwise. Let me check if 2001 is a leap year. To determine if a year is a leap year, the rule is: if the year is divisible by 4, it's a leap year, unless it's a century year (divisible by 100) unless it's also divisible by 400. So 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, which means it's not divisible by 4. Therefore, 2001 is not a leap year, so the last day of February 2001 would be February 28, 2001. So Jane was born on 02/28/2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date is her 16th birthday. Since she was born in 2001, adding 16 years would take us to 2017. Wait, 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today's date is the same day she was born, but in 2017. So today is 02/28/2017, right? Because she was born on the last day of February, which in 2001 was 02/28, and since 2017 is not a leap year (2017 divided by 4 is 504.25, so not a leap year), the last day of February 2017 is also 02/28.
